methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Valthos is a Seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Kathleen McMahon and Tess van Stekelenburg that has raised $30M to date. The valuation analysis triangulates across a universe of 10 private and 2 public comparable companies in the Defense & Security sector, including same-stage and adjacent-stage peers such as Tenna Systems, Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries.

Two valuation methods were applied. The Stage-Progression method uses the $30M total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applies a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier for progression from Seed to the next financing stage, yielding $75M. The Peer-Median method examines four same-sector comparables—three Series A companies valued between $150M and $500M and one Seed-stage peer at $60M—producing a median valuation of $225M with a range spanning $60M to $500M.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $64M (low) to $259M (high), with a median of $150M. This wide spread reflects material uncertainty around Valthos's relative positioning within its peer set. Key sensitivities include the company's revenue traction and customer concentration relative to Series A comparables, which command substantially higher valuations. The absence of public Defense & Security pure-plays with direct operational similarity further limits precision in comparable benchmarking.
